publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-03-10 23:56:47 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, diaries, film, printed materials, legal and financial documents.
did.abstract: Collection includes correspondence with friends and family members, diaries, scrapbooks, and published materials concerning Briehl's activism, communism, socialism, agriculture, pacifism, and radical causes

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Fred Briehl was a dairy farmer and summer guest house operator, Wallkill, Ulster County; political party and union organizer; accountant, printer and editor of THE FARM FRONT.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes correspondence with friends and family members, 1911-1947 (1911-1920 bulk); diaries (1917-1920) written primarily when Briehl was in prison for refusing to serve in the armed forces during World War I; correspondence with various politicians and the media concerning current affairs, 1917-1970; legal documents pertaining to lawsuits, property and patents, 1942-1961, and the 1955 House Un-American Activities Committee investigation of summer camps; tape recordings of interviews and events, 1962-1978; videotapes of home movies taken by Briehl, a clip from "Native Land," a 1942 documentary on civil rights in which Briehl acted, and a documentary film, "Fred Briehl" by Charles Gershwin and Michael Mitchell, containing clips from his home movies and interviews during 1970-1971; student notebooks and papers, 1914-1920; scrapbooks and clippings, 1915-1974; correspondence, broadsides, handouts and speeches relating to activities in the Communist Party and the Dairyman's League, the Farmer's Union, and the Farmer's Emergency Committee; records from his dairy farm, 1927-1944; issues of THE FARM FRONT, published by the New York Farm Commission of the Communist Party, (1943-1959) and edited by or with Briehl; pamphlets and miscellaneous publications concerning communism, socialism, agriculture, pacifism, and radical causes.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Fred Briehl. Papers, #3474. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Farmers.
Radicalism.
Pacifism.
Agriculture.
Socialism.
Communism.
Civil rights.
World War, 1914-1918 -- Conscientious objectors.
controlaccess.title: The farm front.
controlaccess.corpname: New York Commission of the Communist Party
United States. Congress. House Un-American Activities Committee

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.
